title: Boat Rental in Naples: 2026 Guide to Costs, Licences & Best Routes
html:
Exploring the Bay of Naples by boat offers an unforgettable perspective on this Italian coastline. Departing from Molo Beverello, you can rent a motorboat for around 350 EUR for a half day or book a skippered charter for a full excursion to Capri. BoatHire24 connects you directly with local boat owners, making it easy to compare verified listings and secure your perfect day on the water.

Where to set off in Naples

Several well-served marinas and ports provide convenient starting points for your boating adventure. The main passenger port is Molo Beverello, located right in the historic centre near Piazza del Plebiscito, offering direct access to ferries and private charters. For smaller rented boats, consider Marina della Lobra in Sorrento, which is sheltered and close to the scenic Punta Campanella. Other reliable options include Marina di Portosalvo, Mergellina (a busy hub for private vessels) and the charming Marina di Cassano in the Phlegraean Fields, which puts you within reach of Procida and Ischia.

Which boat to rent

Choosing the right boat depends on your group size and the coastal distances you want to cover. The following list outlines the most popular types available through BoatHire24.

  1. Small motorboat (4-6 guests), Best for couples or small families exploring coves like Cala del Lono, from 350 EUR half-day. For a reliable example, check the Olympic 490 for hire, a compact speedboat that seats up to 5.
  2. RIB (6-8 guests), Fast and agile, ideal for reaching Capri (12 nm) in under 45 minutes, from 450 EUR half-day.
  3. Sailing yacht (8-10 guests), A relaxed way to cruise the Gulf, with overnight berths available, from 600 EUR full day.
  4. Catamaran (10-12 guests), Spacious and stable, perfect for larger parties or longer voyages to Ischia, from 800 EUR full day.
  5. Crewed luxury motor yacht (12+ guests), Includes a skipper and often a host, ideal for weddings or corporate events, from 1500 EUR full day. To compare skippered options, you can find a skippered charter directly on the platform.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Naples?

Prices range from 50 EUR per person for a shared tour to over 2000 EUR for a crewed yacht. The cost depends on boat type, duration, and season. Sharing a guided group excursion from Molo Beverello starts at about 50 EUR per person for a two-hour tour. Renting a small motorboat (up to 20 ft) for a half day typically costs between 350 and 500 EUR. A full-day catamaran charter for up to 12 guests averages 800 to 1200 EUR. For a crewed luxury yacht with a skipper, expect to pay from 1500 EUR and up on a full-day basis. Always check the exact boat details and verify whether fuel, mooring fees and insurance are included.

  • Shared tour (2 h, up to 12 pax): from 50 EUR per person.
  • Small motorboat (half day, 5 pax): from 350 EUR.
  • Catamaran (full day, 12 pax): from 800 EUR.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (full day, 12+ pax): from 1500 EUR.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Naples?

For boats over 40 hp, a valid boating licence is required for self-drive rental in Italian waters. Italian law requires a patente nautica for motorboats with engine power exceeding 40 hp. Many small motorboats under that threshold (such as the Olympic 490) can be driven without a licence, but the operator must be over 18 and have basic navigational knowledge. Always confirm the specific requirements with the boat owner on BoatHire24 before booking. For skippered charters, the skipper holds the necessary licence, so you need no qualification. How boat rental works on the platform includes clear licensing details for every listing, making it easy to choose the right option for your skill level.

Naples offers calm sea conditions for most of the high season (June to September), but watch for the Libeccio wind, which can pick up in the afternoon and create choppy seas around the Sorrento peninsula. A local tip: start your trip early, before 10 am, to enjoy flat water and quieter anchorages.

At a glance

  • Price range: 50, 2000+ EUR per trip
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: 2 hours (shared) to full day (8 hours)
  • Typical capacity: 4, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Departure ports: Molo Beverello, Mergellina, Marina della Lobra, Marina di Stabia

Questions about boat rental in Naples

Can I rent a boat in Naples without a boating licence?

Yes, if you choose a skippered charter or a small motorboat under 40 hp, no licence is needed.

How long does it take to get from Naples to Capri by boat?

A fast RIB or motorboat covers the 12 nautical miles in about 40 to 50 minutes in calm conditions.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Naples?

The high season from June to September offers the warmest weather and calmest seas, but May and October can also be pleasant with fewer crowds.

Are there any hidden costs when renting a boat?

Fuel, mooring fees, and cleaning charges are sometimes extra; always check the listing details on BoatHire24.

To secure your chosen vessel for 2026, browse the full selection of boats in Naples on BoatHire24 and check availability directly with owners. If you have a boat to rent out, the marketplace makes it simple to list your own and reach thousands of travellers looking for authentic boating experiences in the Bay of Naples.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Naples and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
